title: PTD Today — Media
disclaimer: Informational only — AI-generated summaries; may contain errors. Verify with the original video.

items.title: When your goals need backup, Copilot shows up.

ai.summary: Based on the available description… The video, titled "When your goals need backup, Copilot shows up." and published by Microsoft Copilot, positions Copilot as an assistant for when extra support is needed. It invites viewers to download and try Copilot via the link in the bio.
bullets: Title frames Copilot as support when goals need backup.
Clear call to action: "Download & try Copilot" via link in bio.
Published by the Microsoft Copilot channel.
No transcript is available.
Content is CTA-focused and promotional.
takeaways: Awareness: Microsoft markets Copilot as a tool to provide additional support when objectives require it.
Action: Use the provided link to download and trial Copilot to determine relevance for your operations.
Evaluation: A hands-on trial is the primary way to assess whether Copilot can help meet specific operational goals.

items.title: The Most Expensive Mistake in AI (And How to Avoid It)

ai.summary: Based on the available description, Marcus Oliver of Google Delta (Google Cloud professional services) argues the most expensive AI mistake is rushing to code without a strategy — not compute costs. He advises escaping the “coding trap” by reimagining value chains and deploying multi-agent systems to drive real business outcomes.
bullets: Most costly AI mistake: rushing to code without a clear strategy, not compute spending.
Marcus Oliver, lead of Google Delta professional services, explains the issue.
Businesses should focus on reimagining value chains rather than immediate implementation.
Deploying multi-agent systems can help drive tangible business outcomes.
Episode is part of The AI Vantage series from Google Cloud.
takeaways: Prioritize strategic planning over immediate code/compute investments to maximize ROI.
Align AI projects with redesigned value chains to ensure infrastructure decisions support outcomes.
Evaluate investments in multi-agent capabilities as a means to deliver measurable business value.
Engage professional services or strategy expertise early to avoid costly, misaligned builds.

ai.summary: Based on the available description… This replayed kickoff episode from PwC’s Reporting reset series reviews foundational reporting principles, key disclosure considerations, and distinctions between public and private company financial statements. It covers balance sheet and income statement presentation, determining reporting periods, accounting changes and error corrections, and subsequent events, with timestamps and links to PwC guidance.
bullets: Overview of foundational GAAP and SEC requirements for financial statement presentation.
Guidance on selecting appropriate reporting periods and related considerations.
Balance sheet presentation: classification, required disclosures, and best practices.
Income statement presentation: structure and key considerations.
Discussion of accounting changes, estimates, error corrections, and subsequent events.
takeaways: Confirm balance sheet classification and disclosures for capital assets and liabilities to ensure accurate representation of data center investments.
Verify income statement presentation for revenue and operating expenses tied to grid/data center operations to support stakeholder transparency.
Review reporting periods and subsequent events processes to capture material events (e.g., major outages, acquisitions) that affect year-end reporting.
Document and disclose any accounting changes, estimate revisions, or error corrections promptly to maintain audit readiness and regulatory compliance.

items.title: DeepKeep: Secure your AI agents and applications end-to-end

ai.summary: Based on the available description, DeepKeep on Google Cloud is a unified platform to secure AI agents, applications, and user interactions, offering visibility to eliminate shadow AI and protect infrastructure from evolving threats. It highlights three core capabilities—AI Lens for interaction visibility and policy enforcement, AI Red Teaming for stress-testing against real-world attacks, and an AI Firewall for real-time prompt/response guardrails.
bullets: Unified platform on Google Cloud to secure AI agents, applications, and user interactions.
AI Lens: visibility and policy application across user interactions with AI systems.
AI Red Teaming: stress-test applications and models against real-world attacks.
AI Firewall: real-time security for prompts and responses with built-in guardrails.
Designed to eliminate shadow AI and protect infrastructure from evolving threats.
takeaways: Visibility and policy enforcement help reduce unapproved AI deployments (shadow AI) that can introduce operational and security risk.
Regular red teaming can surface model and application vulnerabilities before they are exploited, improving resilience.
Real-time prompt/response firewalling and guardrails reduce the chance of unsafe outputs impacting systems or data.
A single, cloud-native platform can streamline policy enforcement and incident response across distributed AI workloads.

items.title: On the Edge: World Cup Ski Racing | Ryan Cochran-Siegle

ai.summary: Based on the available description… The video follows U.S. Ski Team racer Ryan Cochran-Siegle, emphasizing that an Olympic legacy raises the standard but does not replace hard work. It promotes ESPN Originals’ On the Edge: World Cup Ski Racing and directs viewers to teamusa.com/tv and Team USA’s social channels.
bullets: Features Ryan Cochran-Siegle and the U.S. Ski Team.
Central message: "Nothing is given, everything is earned."
Part of ESPN Originals’ On the Edge: World Cup Ski Racing series.
Available to watch at teamusa.com/tv.
Promotes Team USA channels on YouTube, Facebook, Twitter, Instagram, and TikTok.
takeaways: The description lists multiple distribution platforms (teamusa.com/tv and major social channels), so plan capacity for multi-channel delivery.
Ensure CDN scalability and caching strategies to handle concentrated demand when promoting episodic sports content.
Implement robust monitoring and redundancy to maintain availability during new releases and promotional traffic peaks.
Protect content integrity and brand presence across platforms with appropriate security and authentication controls.

Decision-makers cannot derive actionable implications from this description alone.

items.title: GeForce Garage - Summer of RTX Community Build

ai.summary: Based on the available description, NVIDIA's GeForce Garage highlights the Summer of RTX r/buildapc Community Build winner, u/JeffersonPutnam, who assembled a small‑form‑factor PC centered on a GeForce RTX 5090 Founders Edition. The description lists the full parts used (Fractal Design Era 2, MSI MPG B850I Edge, Ryzen 7 9800X3D, 32GB DDR5‑6000, Corsair SF1000, Corsair Nautilus 240 RS) and links to the PCPartPicker build and GeForce social channels.
bullets: Winner: u/JeffersonPutnam’s SFF GeForce RTX 5090 build for the Summer of RTX community contest.
Host: GeForce host Dwight (Twitter: @_MightyPotato) featured in the video.
Chassis and GPU: Fractal Design Era 2 with a GeForce RTX 5090 Founders Edition.
Key components: MSI MPG B850I Edge motherboard, Ryzen 7 9800X3D CPU, 32GB TeamGroup DDR5‑6000, Corsair SF1000 PSU, Corsair Nautilus 240 RS AIO.
Resources: Full parts list on PCPartPicker and GeForce social links provided in the description.
takeaways: Demonstrates that a GeForce RTX 5090 Founders Edition can be integrated into a compact Fractal Era 2 SFF chassis with appropriate power and cooling choices.
Specified power and cooling (Corsair SF1000 PSU and Nautilus 240 RS AIO) indicate the supporting infrastructure needed to run an RTX 5090 in a small form factor.
Platform pairing (MSI MPG B850I Edge + Ryzen 7 9800X3D + 32GB DDR5‑6000) shows a balance of high‑performance CPU/memory with a flagship GPU for workstation or edge workstation-style deployments.
PCPartPicker parts list provides an exact bill of materials useful for procurement or repeatable builds.
